title: Lewis Needs A Kidney

jokes.text: Do me a favor, tell me the details of your last conversation. / That's none of your business! / Exactly.
jokes.setup: Larry refuses to tell security what his call is about because it's personal
jokes.punchline: He uses the same logic to refuse their invasive question, highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - using their own invasive behavior to illustrate his point. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: Schmuck! Fucking idiot!
jokes.setup: Larry berating himself for locking keys in car
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top self-flagellation for minor mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-abuse, character-specific but predictable

jokes.text: Sir, are you in a car? / No. / Sir, this is a drive-thru line. You have to be in a car to order.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to order food on foot at a drive-thru
jokes.punchline: The rigid enforcement of drive-thru policy despite the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb premise - arbitrary rules creating absurd situations. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I tell you, Pete, you should have got the jumbo jack... The next time you come through, go for the bacon bacon cheeseburger and the asian salad. A strange combination, but it goes down easy, and it comes out fast. I don't need to tell you more.
jokes.setup: Larry gives unsolicited detailed food advice to a stranger
jokes.punchline: The overly intimate digestive details to someone he just met
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry behavior - inappropriate oversharing with strangers. The 'I don't need to tell you more' while having told too much is classic.

jokes.text: Really? What does somebody do working alongside a dental hygienist? / I watched and worked closely with a dental hygienist.
jokes.setup: Larry asks what Pete did working alongside a dental hygienist
jokes.punchline: Pete's non-answer that's just a restatement of the original phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic joke. Pete's inability to explain his job beyond repeating the title is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: you got some porno back there? / Yeah... well no, it's not mine. That was... that was left by a couple of my co-workers... I had no idea that that was there.
jokes.setup: Larry notices porn in Pete's car and asks about it
jokes.punchline: Pete's obviously false denial with increasingly desperate explanations
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward denial humor. The 'co-workers left it' excuse is predictable but still effective.

jokes.text: Louis Lewis? / Louis Lewis. / Please. / What about him? / Might as well be called Manson Manson.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Richard's cousin with the redundant name Louis Lewis
jokes.punchline: Richard's comparison to 'Manson Manson' - playing on the name repetition while making him sound like a serial killer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant name joke that connects redundant naming to criminal associations. The 'Manson Manson' construct is inspired.

jokes.text: He says he wants to donate all of his organs after he dies, but not a minute before.
jokes.setup: Richard explains his cousin's position on organ donation
jokes.punchline: The selfish precision of 'not a minute before' death
jokes.explanation: Great specificity in the selfishness. The 'not a minute before' adds perfect comedic precision to the organ donation hypocrisy.

jokes.text: The next time there's any sign of anything wrong with any of my friends... if I think they're gonna be going to the hospital, any sign of anything, I'm just... I'm just gonna drop them like a hot potato.
jokes.setup: Richard contemplates how to avoid future kidney requests
jokes.punchline: His plan to preemptively abandon friends at the first sign of illness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly selfish Richard Lewis thinking. The preemptive friend abandonment strategy is hilariously dark.

jokes.text: we're losing the sickness and health clause. / I'm out if anything's wrong with you... And look, same for you... I can't be around illness. Freaks me out, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl discuss their marriage vows
jokes.punchline: Their mutual agreement to abandon each other during illness, treating marriage like a business contract
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of marriage vows. The businesslike negotiation of abandonment is perfectly crafted cringe.

jokes.text: I know, but your snuggle always leads to sex. So what's wrong with that? Why shouldn't it lead to sex?
jokes.setup: Cheryl wants to snuggle without sex
jokes.punchline: Larry genuinely confused why anyone would want intimacy without sex
jokes.explanation: Classic male perspective humor, character-specific but familiar territory

jokes.text: The ass is part of my snuggle... It is! / Okay, then good night. / It's just... it's just a lever. I'm using the ass as a lever to draw you in. That's all. and it gets... the snuggle tighter.
jokes.setup: Cheryl objects to Larry's hands during snuggling
jokes.punchline: Larry's mechanical explanation of using her ass as a 'lever' for better snuggling efficiency
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Larry logic. Reducing romantic intimacy to mechanical engineering is inspired absurdist comedy.

jokes.text: You asked me what it was regarding! I didn't know it was going to be about something like this! But I... I wouldn't have said anything! You asked me!
jokes.setup: Larry accidentally reveals Richard's kidney problem to his assistant
jokes.punchline: His defensive justification that becomes increasingly frantic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier security conversation. Larry's frantic justification escalates perfectly.

jokes.text: Let me just say this: I would have gone on record at the deli.
jokes.setup: Richard criticizes Larry for not immediately offering to give a kidney
jokes.punchline: Richard's claim he would have dramatically announced his kidney donation at a deli
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard Lewis grandiosity. The specificity of 'at the deli' makes his theatrical gesture even more absurd.

jokes.text: If you're married to your wife for three years and I've known her for 20, who should give your wife the kidney? I've known her longer.
jokes.setup: Larry making an analogy about kidney donation priority
jokes.punchline: Taking the longevity argument to an absurd extreme
jokes.explanation: Excellent reductio ad absurdum that exposes the flaw in Jeff's logic

jokes.text: If my wife needs a kidney, you can give it to her. / Oh, okay, so it's longevity no matter what, even if you're married to her. / No, I'm saying you seemed like you want to give her a kidney, so I'm gonna let you.
jokes.setup: Jeff argues Larry should give the kidney based on friendship longevity
jokes.punchline: Larry's willingness to let Jeff give his own wife a kidney based on Jeff's logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reductio ad absurdum of Jeff's friendship logic. Larry's casual willingness to trade his wife's kidney is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You know, I'm loathe to mention this, okay, but you are an employee of mine.
jokes.setup: Larry is losing the kidney donation argument with Jeff
jokes.punchline: His desperate pull of the employer card as a last resort
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Larry's desperation. The 'loathe to mention' followed by the employer power play is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: It's kind of a long story, but I had a burger with a... at a jack in the box with some guy and now I'm his alibi.
jokes.setup: Omar asks why Larry is at a police station
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual explanation of how a burger led to being an alibi for a criminal
jokes.explanation: Great casual delivery of an absurd situation. The matter-of-fact tone about becoming a criminal's alibi is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm a private eye, not a psychologist.
jokes.setup: Omar dealing with his traumatized receptionist
jokes.punchline: Professional boundary complaint about emotional labor
jokes.explanation: Good professional frustration line with nice economy

jokes.text: And I say 'I'm planning on killing myself. I want to talk to him.' / There's protocol.
jokes.setup: Larry making an analogy about receptionist protocols
jokes.punchline: Extreme example followed by Omar's bureaucratic response
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective escalation of the protocol argument

jokes.text: I changed my mind. / Really? / Yeah. / Wow. / I'll take the test. Who knows?
jokes.setup: Jeff suddenly deciding to take the kidney test during lineup
jokes.punchline: Peer pressure working at the worst possible timing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's competitive nature

jokes.text: That's a late dinner. / My mother used to call it a late night snack. / Dinner's between 5:00 and 8:00 generally... You could start at 8:00 though.
jokes.setup: Detective questions the timing of Larry's 10:15 PM meal
jokes.punchline: Larry's pedantic negotiation of dinner timing during a police interrogation
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry priority confusion - focusing on meal terminology during serious police business. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You two gentlemen shared a meal with number two on the 23rd... / Let him go. You're done here, gentlemen.
jokes.setup: The detective builds up the serious criminal they've been helping
jokes.punchline: Immediate release after confirming the alibi, showing how pointless the whole exercise was
jokes.explanation: Solid anticlimax structure. The bureaucratic buildup to immediate dismissal works well.

jokes.text: Both of your blood types are compatible with Mr. Lewis for his kidney transplant. Good night, nurse.
jokes.setup: Doctor delivers serious medical news
jokes.punchline: Larry responds with inappropriate old-timey exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's awkward response to serious news

jokes.text: Good night, nurse. / Good night. / It's just a saying. / I never heard of it. / It's an old one.
jokes.setup: Jeff uses an old expression 'Good night, nurse'
jokes.punchline: The extended awkward explanation of an outdated phrase
jokes.explanation: Mild generational humor about outdated expressions. The extended explanation kills the momentum.

jokes.text: That's nice. / I know it is. / Do you? / Do you? / I do. / Good for you. / It is good for me. / Oh you think so? / I know so. / I'm glad. / So am I. / That makes two of us. / So you say. / So I did.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the old saying
jokes.punchline: Increasingly circular and competitive agreement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation into absurd competitive agreement - perfect Larry and Jeff dynamic

jokes.text: Me play you in bingo? That's like asking me to play golf against Tiger Woods.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests they play bingo to decide who gives the kidney
jokes.punchline: Larry comparing Jeff's bingo skills to Tiger Woods' golf prowess
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist comparison. Treating bingo as requiring Tiger Woods-level skill is perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm more likely to do it than you?
jokes.setup: Jeff warns Larry not to campaign for the kidney choice
jokes.punchline: Larry's indignant reaction implying Jeff is more likely to cheat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's competitive defensiveness. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Well, what's it regarding? / I don't know. / You didn't ask? / Nope.
jokes.setup: Larry asking what Lewis and Jeff are discussing
jokes.punchline: The assistant learned not to ask after previous trauma
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing character growth from trauma

jokes.text: Touching hair is part of my consolation routine. / No, it's really weird. / You're being really inappropriate.
jokes.setup: Larry defends touching the crying assistant's hair
jokes.punchline: His clinical description of hair-touching as part of a 'consolation routine'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry misunderstanding of appropriate behavior. The 'consolation routine' phrasing is hilariously clinical.

jokes.text: Touching hair is part of my consolation routine.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his consolation technique
jokes.punchline: Describing intimate gestures as a 'routine'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to snuggling scene with Larry's mechanical view of intimacy

jokes.text: Same thing happened with Cheryl. She said she was leaving. I thought she was leaving me. She's just going out to have lunch with her friend.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to distinguish between 'leaving' and 'quitting'
jokes.punchline: His paranoid misinterpretation of his wife's lunch plans as abandonment
jokes.explanation: Good insight into Larry's neurotic relationship anxiety. The casual delivery of deep insecurity works well.

jokes.text: And I'm not gonna... I don't want to get into that whole game. But, you know, he's... there's a difference when you're younger, obviously... His are bigger too. It's a big hefty kidney, could just handle a big load I think, you know?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to subtly campaign for why Jeff should give the kidney
jokes.punchline: His increasingly graphic description of Jeff's kidney capacity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry hypocrisy - immediately breaking his no-campaigning promise with increasingly inappropriate arguments.

jokes.text: His are bigger too. It's a big hefty kidney, could just handle a big load I think, you know? You could start drinking again if you wanted to with that thing.
jokes.setup: Larry campaigning about Jeff's kidney quality
jokes.punchline: Describes Jeff's kidneys like industrial equipment, suggests alcohol capacity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - treating organs like consumer products with performance specs

jokes.text: You could start drinking again if you wanted to with that thing.
jokes.setup: Larry continues selling Jeff's kidney benefits
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Richard could resume drinking with Jeff's superior kidney
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant logic - treating the kidney transplant as an upgrade that enables bad habits. Very Larry thinking.

jokes.text: Flip a coin, man. Eeny meeny miney moe. What do I care?
jokes.setup: Lewis's casual attitude toward choosing his kidney donor
jokes.punchline: Treating life-saving surgery selection like a children's game
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lewis's detachment from the gravity

jokes.text: No, I don't think I'm a match anyway. Let's just do eeny meeny.
jokes.setup: Jeff declining to take the kidney test
jokes.punchline: Claiming he wouldn't be a match while participating in donor selection
jokes.explanation: Good character inconsistency showing Jeff's strategic avoidance

jokes.text: How come you want me to be eeny? / No, it's good. You should be eeny. / Want me to be eeny? Do you want me to be eeny?
jokes.setup: Deciding who should be 'eeny' in eeny meeny miney moe
jokes.punchline: Paranoid analysis of the strategic implications
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid escalation but could be stronger

jokes.text: Eeny meeny miney moe, catch a tiger by the toe. If he hollers, let him go. My mother says to pick this one and out goes y-o-U. / Yeah yeah yeah! / What are you excited about? / I won! / No, you lost! You're out. You're it.
jokes.setup: They use eeny meeny miney moe to decide who gives the kidney
jokes.punchline: Larry's celebration of 'winning' when he actually lost and has to give the kidney
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist escalation. Larry celebrating his own loss because he doesn't understand the game is brilliantly crafted.

jokes.text: No, you lost! You're out. You're it. No no no no no. Out goes y-o-U. You're the loser. No, you're the loser. Tell him, tell him, tell him.
jokes.setup: Confusion over eeny meeny miney moe rules
jokes.punchline: Grown men arguing like children over playground game results
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation showing adult regression under pressure

jokes.text: What are we talking about anyway? Losing what? / They're giving a kidney to Richard Lewis. / Excuse me, you're not giving your fucking kidney.
jokes.setup: Susie discovering what they're arguing about
jokes.punchline: Susie's immediate and forceful intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie intervention with characteristic forceful logic

jokes.text: He needs his kidney, number one. Number two, he's a fat fuck. He can't even survive the surgery... You are healthy and thin. You're giving the kidney. End of discussion, moot point.
jokes.setup: Susie arrives during the kidney argument
jokes.punchline: Her brutal but logical assessment that settles the argument instantly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie entrance - cutting through the nonsense with brutal efficiency. The 'fat fuck' assessment is harshly logical.

jokes.text: I take care of myself all these years... the diet, the exercise, the whole thing, for what? I'd have been better off just eating crap and smoking. That's what I should have been doing all these years... eating crap and smoking.
jokes.setup: Larry's healthy lifestyle backfired - now he has to give kidney
jokes.punchline: Regrets healthy living, wishes he'd been unhealthy and ineligible
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal - good health becomes punishment

jokes.text: Maybe this is why you were saved when you were drowning that day. Maybe this is... the reason. / Oh really? / Yeah. / Oh, he saved me so I can give up a kidney? That's why? If he wants to get so involved, why doesn't he just fix his kidney tonight while he's sleeping... instead of going through all this drowning-saving business?
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggests Larry was saved from drowning to help Richard
jokes.punchline: Larry's complaint about God's inefficient methods - questioning why God doesn't just fix the kidney directly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant theological complaint. Larry's practical critique of God's methods is perfectly characteristic and hilariously irreverent.

jokes.text: If he wants to get so involved, why doesn't he just fix his kidney tonight while he's sleeping... instead of going through all this drowning-saving business?
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggests divine intervention saved Larry to help Richard
jokes.punchline: Larry questions God's inefficient methodology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant theological complaint - Larry arguing with God's project management

jokes.text: You're turning consolation into sex? / Well, that's the ultimate form of consolation. / No, it's not. / Yeah, sure it is.
jokes.setup: Cheryl objects to Larry's sexual advance during consolation
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact argument that sex is the ultimate consolation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the earlier consolation theme. Larry's logical but inappropriate reasoning is spot-on character work.

jokes.text: Hey, that's that guy Pete... the guy I got out of jail. / The victim of the shooting in this case, a Mr. Louis Lewis... cousin of actor-comedian Richard Lewis, is in a coma and is not expected to survive.
jokes.setup: Larry recognizes Pete on the news as a criminal
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Louis Lewis, the kidney-hoarding cousin, is Pete's victim
jokes.explanation: Masterful convergence of multiple plot threads. The irony that the kidney-hoarding cousin becomes the victim is perfectly constructed dark comedy.

jokes.text: Hey, that's that guy Pete... the guy I got out of jail. The victim of the shooting in this case, a Mr. Louis Lewis... cousin of actor-comedian Richard Lewis, is in a coma and is not expected to survive.
jokes.setup: Pete committed Jack in the Box robbery
jokes.punchline: Victim is Louis Lewis - the cousin who wouldn't donate kidney
jokes.explanation: Masterful convergence of all episode threads - dark cosmic justice for selfish cousin

tmdb_overview: When Richard Lewis finds out he needs a kidney transplant, he finds two friends who match him, leaving them to decide who ""gets"" to give up their organ.
